description: Range.containsPosition uses >= instead of > for the endColumn boundary check. The documented contract says positions at the edge are included ('If the position is at the edges, will return true'), but >= excludes positions at exactly the endColumn. This breaks find-and-replace highlighting, selection containment, bracket matching, code lens, and virtually every editor feature that checks whether a position falls within a range.

createRegExp matchCase flag inverted — the 'i' (case-insensitive) modifier is added when matchCase is TRUE instead of when it is FALSE. This means case-sensitive searches become case-insensitive and vice versa. This is a cross-file defect: createRegExp in strings.ts is called by SearchParams.parseSearchRequest() in textModelSearch.ts, by richEditBrackets.ts for bracket matching, and by search workers — all search functionality is affected.

isMultilineRegexSource is missing the CharCode.W check for backslash-W (\W) patterns. Since \W matches any non-word character including newlines, regex patterns containing \W should be treated as potentially multiline. Without this check, \W patterns are searched line-by-line instead of across line boundaries, producing silently incorrect search results.

Provider sort order inverted in _compareByScoreAndTime — returns -1 when a._score < b._score (should return 1 for descending order). This causes the lowest-scoring providers to appear first. Combined with the scoring from languageSelector.ts where wildcard '*' scores 5 and exact language match scores 10, this means generic wildcard providers are preferred over language-specific providers, breaking completions, hover, diagnostics, and all language features.

Position.isBefore uses <= instead of < for column comparison. The documented contract states 'If the two positions are equal, the result will be false', but <= returns true when columns are equal (on the same line). This breaks the distinction between isBefore and isBeforeOrEqual, causing off-by-one errors in cursor movement, selection direction detection, and undo/redo operations.

isValidMatch uses OR (||) instead of AND (&&) for word boundary checks. Whole-word matching requires BOTH left AND right boundaries, but OR accepts matches with a boundary on only one side. Searching for 'test' with whole-word matching would match 'testing' (left boundary exists, right does not). This is a cross-file defect: the word separator definitions come from WordCharacterClassifier in wordCharacterClassifier.ts, and the boundary logic in leftIsWordBounday/rightIsWordBounday both need to pass.
